headline: Monaco explosion: The Ukrainian-born oligarch's shady business dealings
description: Vadym Yermolaiev, a wealthy businessman, was targeted by a homemade explosive bomb on Monday. Despite renouncing his Ukrainian nationality, he continued business activities connected to his country of origin.

US Supreme Court blocks White House effort to end birthright citizenship for children of undocumented migrants
description: After his reciprocal tariff policy was struck down in February, Donald Trump has suffered yet another major setback before the nation's highest court. The policy in question was a flagship of his anti-immigration agenda.

headline: Spain has three months to review one million migrants' applications for regularization
description: Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez's government is defending a measure aimed at supporting economic growth amid a 'demographic winter.' This is Spain's seventh such initiative in 40 years. Initially, the plan was met with broad consensus, but it has since become a source of division.

headline: Mbappé fires France into World Cup last 16
description: France's victory over Sweden on Tuesday has elevated prolific striker Kylian Mbappé to an incredible World Cup record: 18 in 18 matches – only one behind Argentina's Lionel Messi's all-time record of 19 goals.

headline: EU judges' opaque handling of potential conflicts of interest
description: Members of the Court of Justice of the European Union have ruled on cases involving companies or economic sectors in which they had interests, according to an investigation conducted since 2024 by Investigate Europe and its partners, including Le Monde.

description: A Russian exile media outlet has analyzed nearly 7,000 letters from Russian soldiers and their families to the Kremlin thanks to a security flaw.

headline: 'The King and the Mockingbird': The animated masterpiece that inspired Studio Ghibli
description: His film took more than three decades to reach the screen. In 1946, Paul Grimault, then 41, began work on 'Le Roi et l'Oiseau' ('The King and the Mockingbird') with Jacques Prévert writing the screenplay. He finally saw it screened in 1979, at 74. The hand-drawn animation became a cult classic, inspiring generations of viewers and filmmakers.

headline: 'We should not accept that heatwaves cause thousands of deaths. That is not normal'
description: In an interview with Le Monde, Julien Dossier, founder of the environmental consulting firm Quattrolibri, offers an initial political assessment of how France responded to the most intense heatwave ever recorded in the country.
